page: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 7559 GB local disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage configurations. No GPU hardware is included.

In benchmark evaluations, the D128ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its multi-core CPU processing capabilities, measured via stress-ng and PassMark, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is similarly robust, with cached read bandwidth reaching 12266.71 GB/sec and large-block uncached write speeds of 188.06 GB/sec. The system also excels in data manipulation, achieving top-tier throughput in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside high-capacity Redis SET operations at 177.38 million operations per second.

Given its high core count, substantial local storage, and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database hosting, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds. The lack of a dedicated GPU represents a tradeoff for deep learning training, but the extensive CPU instruction set, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, mitigates this for standard analytical and vector-based workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a high-capacity, general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Featuring 512.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 7559 GB local disk, this x86_64 architecture server delivers a balanced resource profile without integrated GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place the instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as memory bandwidth and Redis database operations. Its extensive instruction set supports advanced cryptography and vector extensions, making it highly efficient for enterprise-grade workloads. The D128ads_v7 is optimized for large-scale relational databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-bound large language model inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a 128-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 512 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-performance database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose server with massive local storage and top-tier CPU performance for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 128 vCPUs and 4.5 GHz clock speed
512 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4 GB per core ratio
7559 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Excellent fit for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and web serving
Capable of high-speed CPU-based LLM inference and data compression
